source_document: Colorado Early Learning & Development Guidelines Ages 3-5.pdf
notes: CO is a 3-level document: Domain → Strand → Indicator. There is NO sub_strand. The strands are introduced with a numeric prefix ('1. Relationships with Adults and Peers: ...') which the old detector mistakenly classified as sub_strand because it pattern-matched 'numeric prefix = sub_strand'.

doc_depths.example: 1. Relationships with Adults and Peers: The healthy relationships and interactions with adults and peers.
1. Possess good overall health, including oral, visual, and auditory health, and be free from communicable or preventable diseases.

elements.title: Health, Safety and Nutrition
elements.description: The maintenance of healthy and age appropriate physical well-being, and understanding of healthy and safe habits and practicing healthy habits.

elements.title: Possess good overall health, including oral, visual, and auditory health, and be free from communicable or preventable diseases.
Relationships with Adults and Peers
elements.description: The healthy relationships and interactions with adults and peers.

regression_cases.description: CO has zero sub_strands. Every numbered section header (e.g. '1. Relationships ...') is a strand.
regression_cases.assertion: the detected element list contains zero elements with level == 'sub_strand'.

regression_cases.description: Numeric-prefixed section headers under a domain are strands, not sub_strands or indicators.
regression_cases.assertion: every detected element whose source_text matches r'^\d+\.\s+[A-Z].*:' has level == 'strand'.

regression_cases.description: Every indicator's parent in the parser output must be a strand (sub_strand_code is null).
regression_cases.assertion: for every indicator in the parser output, sub_strand_code is null and strand_code is non-null.
